title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for timing, resource graphs, transfer, caching, and user-performance boundaries and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, network, DNS, parser, filesystem, privacy, accessibility, legal-review, scale, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded page-resource and timing audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Separate queue, DNS, connect, TLS, request, first-byte, body, parse, render, and total timing with clock and instrumentation limits.
Classify document, stylesheet, script, image, font, media, API, iframe, preload, and redirect resources with initiator and origin.
Reconcile compressed transfer, decoded body, cached reuse, duplicate URLs, variant keys, and large-resource policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz User Performance Boundary
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz site-quality team handles case 9730 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate crawler timings, resource graphs, transfer, cache evidence, controlled browser evidence, and user-performance limits; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate crawler timings, controlled browser lab evidence, field data, accessibility, network profile, device profile, and user experience claims.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Response Timing Phases
steps.instruction:
Order the Response Timing Phases Authorized crawling and Python authorization-to-recovery evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
